nodes.label: Two-brain neocortex object
nodes.rationale: One analog chip, two brains: a cheap unsupervised SCFF bulk (~80%) organizes the world and a tiny closed-form namer (~20%) puts labels on it; the P9-frozen committed object Phase 11 measures untouched.

nodes.label: SCFF bulk (nonlinear feature learner)
nodes.rationale: 12 forward-only self-contrastive layers, frozen after Stage 1; it is the nonlinear feature learner — decisive where a linear head can't crack the data (Δbulk +0.417 on synth home, beats a random reservoir), redundant where a linear head already saturates.

nodes.label: Closed-form namer (SLDA)
nodes.rationale: Gradient-free head that reads bulk features and puts labels on them; the continual safety (worst-BWT ~0) largely lives here + the gate + sleep, not in the bulk — the honest answer to 'isn't this just SLDA?'.

nodes.label: Drift gate (DDM)
nodes.rationale: Fires the namer only when the cheap path stalls; a safety mechanism — firing more forgets more.

nodes.label: Sleep loop (grid-4 cadence)
nodes.rationale: Periodic closed-form re-fit over the prototype LUT at grid-4 cadence; consolidates and re-anchors so worst-case retention stays flat.

nodes.label: Hippocampus prototype LUT
nodes.rationale: Bounded CBRS prototype memory feeding sleep re-fit; keeps every class/type represented as the label space grows (all 3 data types alive at 30-way).

nodes.label: Arm A — frozen recipe through the porthole
nodes.rationale: The committed object bit-for-bit (freeze_content identical to P9 hash), forced through a 40-D porthole so every arena enters at the frozen width. Answers: does the thing we committed survive real data?

nodes.label: Arm B — pre-registered scaling recipe
nodes.rationale: Same architecture rebuilt to a size law (D=min(native,160), W=ceil(1.6·D), L=12) declared before any run; answers whether the architecture scales — recovers porthole loss without breaking safety.

nodes.label: ER-strong (per-arena tuned baseline)
nodes.rationale: Experience-replay small net + buffer, the field's strong continual baseline, re-tuned per arena on a disjoint seed-7 stream — the asymmetry runs against the home team on purpose.

nodes.label: worst-BWT (continual safety channel)
nodes.rationale: The worst backward transfer over a run; the object's signature channel (0 = never forgets).

nodes.label: Order-invariance
nodes.rationale: |AA(forward) − AA(reversed)|; small because the namer is closed-form (no gradient path a data ordering can bias) — holds even across a change in the kind of data.

hyperedges.label: The two-brain neocortex object (SCFF bulk + closed-form namer + drift gate + sleep + LUT)
